description: לפני קצת יותר מ-5 שנים נכנסה Tesla לשוק הישראלי. על אף שהיא הספיקה להפוך למכונית החשמלית הכי נמכרת בישראל וגם לצאת לרכבת הרים עם המחירים שלה, רק בשנת 2026 היא מתחילה פעילות טרייד-אין גם בישראל – אבל בדרך שונה. לקבל פחות על הרכב המשומש, אבל לחסוך כאב ראש עסקאות טרייד-אין בענף הרכב מאפשרות לכם לחסוך […]The postטסלה מציעה לראשונה טרייד-אין בישראלappeared first onגיקטיים.
summary_he: Tesla begins offering trade-in services in Israel, but with a twist, allowing customers to save on headaches but potentially receiving lower prices than market value.
details_he: Tesla, after half a decade of activity in Israel, has started offering trade-in services for the first time, but with a different approach than in other countries. In Israel, customers can use the 'Trade Mobil' platform to find and purchase used Teslas, but the process is handled by a third-party company, rather than Tesla itself. This means that customers will not be able to use Tesla's app to get a quote for their used vehicle, pay for the new one, and complete the exchange in a convenient and hassle-free manner. Instead, they will need to contact a third-party company to initiate the trade-in process.

title_original: רם מכנס מונה למנכ"ל הבא של ארבה
description: ייכנס לתפקיד ב-1 באפריל. המנכ"ל הנוכחי, קובי מרנקו, ישמש כנשיא החברה. הגדירה אסטרטגיה חדשה המרחיבה את שוקי היעד וכוללת כניסה לשוק הביטחוני. נסחרת בנסד"ק לפי שווי שוק של כ-100 מיליון דולר
title_he: רם מכנס מונה למנכ"ל הבא של ארבה
summary_he: ייכנס לתפקיד ב-1 באפריל. המנכ"ל הנוכחי, קובי מרנקו, ישמש כנשיא החברה. הגדירה אסטרטגיה חדשה המרחיבה את שוקי היעד וכוללת כניסה לשוק הביטחוני. נסחרת בנסד"ק לפי שווי שוק של כ-100 מיליון דולר

title_original: טילים פגעו בדאטה סנטר של AWS במפרץ
description: כשהדאשבורד של AWS נצבע באדום וערוצי ה-Slack בהיסטריה, הראשון שאנחנו מאשימים (לפחות בראש) זה כנראה ג'וניור תורן שדחף קוד לא בדוק לפרודקשן או איש DevOps שעשה טעות קונפיגורציה קריטית ב-DNS, אבל לפחות הפעם, זו לא אשמת הג'וניור. שירותי הענן של אמזון (AWS) קרסו במספר איזורים, לאחר שאחד מהדאטה-סנטרים של החברה באיחוד האמירויות נפגע כתוצאה מהלחימה […]The postטילים פגעו בדאטה סנטר של AWS במפרץappeared first onגיקטיים.
title_he: טילים פגעו בדאטה סנטר של AWS במפרץ
summary_he: שירותי הענן של אמזון (AWS) קרסו במספר איזורים, לאחר שאחד מהדאטה-סנטרים של החברה באיחוד האמירויות נפגע כתוצאה מהלחימה באזור. האירוע התרחש אתמול, כאשר רסיסי טילים פגעו במתקן וגרמו לשריפה בתוך המבנה.
details_he: שירותי הענן של אמזון (AWS) קרסו במספר איזורים, לאחר שאחד מהדאטה-סנטרים של החברה באיחוד האמירויות נפגע כתוצאה מהלחימה באזור. האירוע התרחש אתמול, כאשר רסיסי טילים פגעו במתקן וגרמו לשריפה בתוך המבנה. כוחות הכיבוי המקומיים שהוזעקו למקום נאלצו לנתק את זרם החשמל הראשי ואת הגנרטורים כדי להשתלט על הלהבות.

title_original: HumanMCP: A Human-Like Query Dataset for Evaluating MCP Tool Retrieval Performance
description:
arXiv:2602.23367v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers contain a collection of thousands of open-source standardized tools, linking LLMs to external systems; however, existing datasets and benchmarks lack realistic, human-like user queries, remaining a critical gap in evaluating the tool usage and ecosystems of MCP servers. Existing datasets often do contain tool descriptions but fail to represent how different users portray their requests, leading to poor generalization and inflated reliability of certain benchmarks. This paper introduces the first large-scale MCP dataset featuring diverse, high-quality diverse user queries generated specifically to match 2800 tools across 308 MCP servers, developing on the MCP Zero dataset. Each tool is paired with multiple unique user personas that we have generated, to capture varying levels of user intent ranging from precise task requests, and ambiguous, exploratory commands, reflecting the complexity of real-world
title_he: HumanMCP: A Human-Like Query Dataset for Evaluating MCP Tool Retrieval Performance
summary_he: Researchers have developed a large-scale dataset called HumanMCP to evaluate the performance of Model Context Protocol (MCP) tool retrieval systems. This dataset contains diverse, high-quality user queries that mimic real-world interaction patterns, allowing for a more accurate assessment of MCP tool usage and ecosystems.
details_he: The HumanMCP dataset was created to address the lack of realistic, human-like user queries in existing MCP datasets and benchmarks. It features 2800 tools across 308 MCP servers, paired with multiple unique user personas that capture varying levels of user intent. This dataset is expected to improve the generalization and reliability of MCP tool retrieval performance evaluations.

title_original: Toward General Semantic Chunking: A Discriminative Framework for Ultra-Long Documents
description:
arXiv:2602.23370v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Long-document topic segmentation plays an important role in information retrieval and document understanding, yet existing methods still show clear shortcomings in ultra-long text settings. Traditional discriminative models are constrained by fixed windows and cannot model document-level semantics; generative large language models can output paragraph boundaries, but inference is expensive and long inputs are difficult to support. To address these issues, we propose a discriminative segmentation model based on Qwen3-0.6B. On top of the backbone network, we add a cross-window context fusion layer and a boundary classification head, and combine them with an overlapping sliding-window strategy. Our model supports single-pass inputs of up to 13k tokens and can be extended to ultra-long documents for paragraph boundary detection. To further enhance downstream retrieval efficiency, we derive a vector fusion method with scalar correction, which
title_he: Toward General Semantic Chunking: A Discriminative Framework for Ultra-Long Documents
summary_he: Researchers at Kaifeng Wu and colleagues have developed a new framework for segmenting ultra-long documents, addressing the limitations of existing methods in processing large texts. Their discriminative model, built on the Qwen3-0.6B backbone network, can handle single-pass inputs of up to 13,000 tokens and supports paragraph boundary detection. This approach significantly improves the efficiency and scalability of long-document processing.
details_he: The proposed framework, Toward General Semantic Chunking, overcomes the shortcomings of traditional discriminative models by incorporating a cross-window context fusion layer and a boundary classification head. It also employs an overlapping sliding-window strategy to enable the processing of ultra-long documents. Furthermore, the researchers have developed a vector fusion method with scalar correction to compress the representation of ultra-long segments into a single vector without semantic loss. Experiments on the Wikipedia long-document topic segmentation dataset WIKI-727K demonstrate that the new framework achieves better macro-averaged F1 scores and delivers two orders of magnitude faster inference compared to generative models based on Qwen2-0.5B.

title_original: The Post-Quantum Journey Begins: Enforce, See, and Evolve with Quantum-Safe SASE
description: Encrypted data has a shelf life, and for many organizations it must remain secret for years. The post-quantum risk is not a dramatic collapse of encryption, but a quieter threat: attackers harvesting encrypted traffic today so they can decrypt it in the future. That is why post-quantum readiness is increasingly a board and CEO-level responsibility,...The postThe Post-Quantum Journey Begins: Enforce, See, and Evolve with Quantum-Safe SASEappeared first onCato Networks.
title_he: The Post-Quantum Journey Begins: Enforce, See, and Evolve with Quantum-Safe SASE
summary_he: As the threat of post-quantum attacks looms, organizations must prioritize long-term business stability, compliance, and trust by preparing for a future where encrypted data can be decrypted. This requires a board-level responsibility, with the CISO leading the execution. A practical first step is to implement TLS 1.3 with post-quantum-cryptography-ready key establishment.
details_he: The post-quantum risk is not a dramatic collapse of encryption, but rather a quieter threat of attackers harvesting encrypted traffic today to decrypt it in the future. This threat demands a coordinated effort to upgrade cryptographic protections, which is where Quantum-Safe SASE comes in. It provides the centralized visibility and enforcement foundation to coordinate these upgrades and apply them gradually, starting where it matters most. The rollout across a complex enterprise requires visibility into where post-quantum cryptography protection is needed and consistent policy enforcement across users, sites, and applications.

title_original: What Defenders Need to Know about Iran’s Cyber Capabilities
description: With the current Iran crisis at its peak, cyber activity is a relevant part of the threat picture alongside kinetic and political pressure. Iran’s ecosystem includes multiple clusters aligned with state entities, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) and the Ministry of Intelligence and Security (MOIS), as well as deniable operators and “hacktivist” groups. This ecosystem supports a broad set of objectives: espionage to gain intelligence and footholds; disruption and destructive activity, including DDoS attacks, pseudo-ransomware, and data wipers to impose costs; and information operations that pair destructive activity or data leaks with coordinated online amplification. This activity is expected to intensify and broaden across the Middle East, the United States, and […]The postWhat Defenders Need to Know about Iran’s Cyber Capabilitiesappeared first onCheck Point Blog.
title_he: What Defenders Need to Know about Iran’s Cyber Capabilities
summary_he: As tensions escalate in the Iran crisis, cyber defenders must be aware of the complex threat landscape posed by Iran's cyber capabilities, which span espionage, disruption, and information operations.
details_he: Iran's cyber ecosystem includes state entities, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, and deniable operators, supporting a range of objectives including espionage, disruption, and information operations. These activities are expected to intensify and broaden across the Middle East, the United States, and other countries viewed as opponents. Key Iranian-linked threat actor clusters, such as Cotton Sandstorm, have been observed using tactics like DDoS attacks, pseudo-ransomware, and data wipers, often paired with information operations to amplify their impact.

title_original: North Korean Hackers Publish 26 npm Packages Hiding Pastebin C2 for Cross-Platform RAT
description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a new iteration of the ongoing Contagious Interview campaign, where the North Korean threat actors have published a set of 26 malicious packages to the npm registry.
The packages masquerade as developer tools, but contain functionality to extract the actual command-and-control (C2) by using seemingly harmless Pastebin content as a dead drop resolver and
title_he: North Korean Hackers Publish 26 npm Packages Hiding Pastebin C2 for Cross-Platform RAT
summary_he: North Korean hackers have been found to have published 26 malicious npm packages under the guise of developer tools, which ultimately drop a credential stealer and remote access trojan after extracting command-and-control URLs from seemingly harmless Pastebin content.
details_he: The packages, which are part of the ongoing Contagious Interview campaign, were published to the npm registry and contain functionality to extract the actual command-and-control (C2) by using Pastebin content as a dead drop resolver. The C2 infrastructure is hosted on Vercel across 31 deployments, and the loader extracts C2 URLs steganographically encoded within three Pastebin pastes. The malicious packages were identified by cybersecurity researchers and include names such as argonist, bcryptance, and bee-quarl, among others, which explicitly declare legitimate packages as dependencies.

title_original: ISC Stormcast For Monday, March 2nd, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9830, (Mon, Mar 2nd)
description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.
title_he: ISC Stormcast For Monday, March 2nd, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9830, (Mon, Mar 2nd)
summary_he: A recent surge in malicious activity has been observed, with attackers leveraging various tactics to compromise systems and steal sensitive information. The threat actors are employing a range of techniques, including phishing and exploitation of vulnerabilities. This has resulted in a significant increase in reported incidents.
details_he: The malicious activity has been linked to a number of high-profile attacks, with multiple organizations reporting breaches and data theft. The attackers are using sophisticated tactics, including spear phishing and zero-day exploits, to evade detection and gain unauthorized access to systems. The surge in malicious activity has put a strain on incident response teams, who are working to contain the damage and prevent further breaches.

title_original: ClawJacked attack let malicious websites hijack OpenClaw to steal data
description: Security researchers have disclosed a high-severity vulnerability dubbed "ClawJacked" in the popular AI agent OpenClaw that allowed a malicious website to silently bruteforce access to a locally running instance and take control over it. [...]
title_he: ClawJacked attack let malicious websites hijack OpenClaw to steal data
summary_he: Security researchers have revealed a critical flaw in the OpenClaw AI platform that enables malicious websites to silently gain control over locally running instances and steal sensitive data. This vulnerability, known as 'ClawJacked,' was discovered by Oasis Security and has since been patched in version 2026.2.26. The issue arises from OpenClaw's default configuration, which exposes a WebSocket interface to localhost.
details_he: The OpenClaw gateway service binds to localhost by default, making it vulnerable to exploitation by malicious websites. Browser cross-origin policies do not block WebSocket connections to localhost, allowing attackers to silently open a connection to the local gateway and attempt authentication without triggering any warnings. Oasis Security found that they could brute-force the OpenClaw management password at hundreds of attempts per second without failed attempts being throttled or logged. Once the correct password is guessed, the attacker can register as a trusted device, as the gateway automatically approves device pairings from localhost without user confirmation.
